intro: An abbreviated history of crypto art — from the first tributes etched onto the blockchain to the auctions that put NFTs on the world stage. Adapted from the chronology by artist Martin Lukas Ostachowski.

events.title: Crypto art enters a rebuilding phase
events.description: As of mid-2026 the space remains quiet after years of contraction — surviving "bedroom artists" and core collectors carry the work forward as the market searches for new footing.

events.title: MOCA Town Hall reflects on a perishing year
events.description: At the December Town Hall the community describes 2025 as the year crypto art "as we knew it" receded — activity scarce for weeks at a time and survival hinging on weathering the volatility.

events.title: Art DeCC0 agents launch from a 100-million-word Codex
events.description: After nine months of "datamancy" by Daïm Al-Yad and 260,000+ API calls, MOCA completes the Codex — 105.5 million words of biography, lore, and metadata for the 10,000 Deos — and derives ElizaOS agent profiles from it, bringing the characters online as AI participants in the crypto art movement.

events.title: Art DeCC0s begins building the Codex
events.description: MOCA starts compiling the Codex, a per-Deco compendium of biography, cultural affiliations, favorite crypto artists, and writing style — the foundation for turning all 10,000 PFPs into AI-driven agents.

events.title: MOCA mints Art DeCC0s at 12:12 PM EST
events.description: The 10,000-piece Art DeCC0s PFP collection launches at 0.0242069 ETH, with free mints pre-whitelisted for holders of any MOCA Collection (Genesis, Permanent, DaïmAlYad), MOCAIC 2023 Fundraiser contributors, and MOCA ROOM / ROOMPass owners.

events.title: MOCA announces Art DeCC0s
events.description: MOCA unveils the 10,000-piece Art DeCC0s collection as "an entirely redefined vision for PFPs" — every character and background imbued with uniqueness to push digital identity toward greater expression.

events.title: Group Exhibition: Resbalar (una cosa)
events.description: Group show at CEM Can Felipa, Barcelona, running through January 2025, featuring Agustine Zeguers, Daniel Llaría, and Nazario Díaz.

events.title: Art DeCC0s enters its generative development phase
events.description: Over the spring and summer the team refines and curates the project across hundreds of thousands of artworks and over a terabyte of raw data, evolving a simple graphical interface into an intricate generative pipeline.

events.title: Colborn Bell coins the name "Art DeCC0s"
events.description: MOCA founder Colborn Bell names the project in the groupchat; the casual brainstorm begins to take on a life of its own.

events.title: The spark behind Art DeCC0s
events.description: A groupchat started by Julian Brangold — following a session with founders Colborn Bell and Rene Schmidt and artists Anubis3100, DaïmAlYad, and Untitled.xyz — seeds the "hare-brained, high-octane, half-baked idea" that becomes MOCA's first PFP project.

events.title: Robbie Barrat's AI portrait resells for 150 ETH
events.description: AI Generated Nude Portrait #7, Frame #166 trades for 150 ETH on SuperRare's secondary market.

events.title: NFT Paris draws the core believers
events.description: The Paris conference is markedly smaller and more intimate than 2022's hype-era crowds — a gathering of the movement's remaining faithful.

events.title: Yuga Labs wins Yuga Labs v. Ripps
events.description: A landmark $1.6M judgment against Ryder Ripps sets precedent for trademark and copyright enforcement around NFTs.

events.title: The market fragments across ETH, Bitcoin, and Solana
events.description: Activity spreads multi-chain: Bitcoin Ordinals surge (ripcache's swarm_, Harto's The Golden Ratio), Ethereum's 1/1 market revives, and Solana draws its own scene.

events.title: OpenSea makes creator royalties optional
events.description: Under market pressure, OpenSea stops enforcing artist royalties on secondary sales — a major blow to the on-chain creator protections artists had fought to establish.

events.title: Hermès v. Rothschild (MetaBirkins) verdict
events.description: A New York jury rules against the MetaBirkins NFTs, affirming that buyers acquire tokens tied to their associated content, not deeds divorced from the art.

events.title: First Historic NFT Fest, Barcelona
events.description: DADA gathers pioneers of NFTs and crypto art at the Reial Cercle Artístic in the Gothic Quarter (28–30 October).

events.title: The market turns from euphoria to contraction
events.description: After 2021's highs the market begins a long contraction, as speculative euphoria gives way to a more sober reality.

events.title: First dip in NFT transaction volume
events.description: Weeks after the Beeple sale, NFT trading sees its first volume dip of the year — later read as the start of the comedown from the speculative peak.

events.title: Launch of the Versus Marketplace
events.description: Serverless, on-chain marketplace built on Flow that stores images directly on the blockchain.

events.title: Launch of the Truesy Marketplace
events.description: Curated NFT marketplace focused on personalities and brands, built on Tezos.

events.title: Mad Dog Jones' Replicator sets record for living Canadian artist
events.description: Micah Dowbak's conceptual work sold at Phillips for $4.2M and generates 220 further versions.

events.title: Group Exhibition: Breadcrumbs
events.description: Ethereum crypto art exhibition curated by Kenny Schachter for Galerie Nagel Draxler, Cologne.

events.title: The Non-Fungible Collection by Pak × Sotheby's
events.description: Sotheby's and Nifty Gateway auction anonymous artist Pak's work exploring conceptual scarcity.

events.title: Group Exhibition: CryptoArt at ZKM
events.description: Curated by Daniel Heiss and Margit Rosen at the ZKM Centre for Art and Media, Karlsruhe.

events.title: Group Exhibition: Virtual Niche
events.description: First institutional crypto art exhibition, at UCCA Lab in Beijing, hosted by BlockCreateArt.

events.title: Beeple's EVERYDAYS sells at Christie's
events.description: Beeple (Mike Winkelmann) sells EVERYDAYS: THE FIRST 5000 DAYS for $69,346,250.

events.title: Christie's auctions its first digital-only NFT
events.description: Christie's selects Beeple's EVERYDAYS as its first standalone digital-art auction lot.

events.title: Launch of Hic et Nunc
events.description: Community-driven Tezos marketplace that became a hub for the clean-NFT movement.

events.title: Launch of the B.20 token at Metapalooza
events.description: Metapurse's B.20 explores social tokens and DAOs, backed by Beeple's collection.

events.title: Launch of Art Blocks
events.description: Generative art platform creating deterministic on-chain works on Ethereum.

events.title: Robert Alice's Block 21 sells for $131,250
events.description: Async.art partners with Christie's; the painting + NFT exceed estimate sevenfold.

events.title: First Women of Crypto Art group exhibition
events.description: WOCA's "She Art" in CryptoVoxels featured works from over 30 artists.

events.title: Trevor Jones' Picasso's Bull sets Nifty Gateway record
events.description: Original NFT animation sells for $55,555 to the Museum of Crypto Art founder.

events.title: Launch of Foundation
events.description: Digital art marketplace addressing price fluctuations using the DAI stablecoin.

events.title: Launch of Async Art
events.description: Experimental platform for programmable, layered digital art on Ethereum.

events.title: Launch of the MOCA Collection
events.description: The Museum of Crypto Art begins its rapidly growing collection, fueling creators and creations.

events.title: Launch of Rarible
events.description: Ethereum tokenization platform with a decentralized team across Moscow, LA, and New York.

events.title: Launch of Mintbase
events.description: Ethereum tokenization platform for digital art and collectibles, founded in Berlin.

events.title: Launch of MakersPlace
events.description: Platform for digital originals and limited editions on Ethereum, San Francisco.

events.title: Launch of Autoglyphs
events.description: On-chain generative artwork tokens issued for charity by Larva Labs.

events.title: This Artwork Is Always On Sale
events.description: Simon de la Rouviere's conceptual piece, perpetually for sale via Harberger taxes.

events.title: McCoy's Public Key / Private Key at the Whitney
events.description: Conceptual work exploring museum ownership; enables 50 people to become donors.

events.title: MoCA launches in CryptoVoxels
events.description: Artist Bnoiit.C creates the first virtual museum commemorating the crypto art movement.

events.title: Christie's records first fine-art auction on blockchain
events.description: The $323.1M Ebsworth sale documented on-chain with Artory.

events.title: Launch of CryptoVoxels
events.description: New Zealand-based virtual world that became a primary exhibition space for crypto art.

events.title: Launch of KnownOrigin
events.description: Platform for digital originals and editions on Ethereum, by Manchester's BlockRocket.

events.title: Launch of SuperRare
events.description: Platform for single-edition digital artworks on Ethereum, by Brooklyn's Pixura.

events.title: Kevin Abosch sells Forever Rose for $1M
events.description: A Valentine's Day work, fractionalized via the GIFTO gifting protocol.

events.title: Rare Art Festival & Rare Pepe auction
events.description: Homer Pepe sells for $38,500 at the emblematic New York City festival.

events.title: Launch of OpenSea
events.description: First decentralized exchange and marketplace for digital collectibles enters alpha.

events.title: Launch of DADA
events.description: Founded 2014, launches with Creeps & Weirdos, its first rare digital artworks collection.

events.title: Launch of CryptoKitties
events.description: Collectible cats on Ethereum by Dapper Labs, popularizing the ERC-721 standard.

events.title: Launch of CryptoPunks
events.description: 10,000 limited digital collectibles issued free on Ethereum by Larva Labs.

events.title: Launch of Curio Cards
events.description: Rare digital art cards — one of the first Ethereum-based NFT art projects.

events.title: First Rare Pepes
events.description: The first user- and artist-issued trading cards on Counterparty — arguably the first crypto art.

events.title: Launch of Etheria
events.description: Cyrus Adkisson launches the first decentralized virtual world at Devcon1 in London.

events.title: MAK Vienna buys digital art with Bitcoin
events.description: First art museum to purchase a digital artwork (by Harm van den Dorpel) with Bitcoin.

events.title: Sarah Meyohas launches BitchCoin
events.description: An artwork-backed currency challenging the economics of the art world.

events.title: Rhea Myers tokenizes her soul (MYSOUL)
events.description: Conceptually tokenized on DogeCoin and Counterparty.

events.title: Rhea Myers: This Contract is Art
events.description: A smart contract that nominates itself as art with a click.

events.title: Launch of Monegraph + Seven on Seven
events.description: Kevin McCoy and Anil Dash build Monegraph in a day, minting what is widely cited as the first NFT.

events.title: Tatiana Moroz launches TatianaCoin
events.description: An early personal/artist token on Counterparty, funding an album.

events.title: Kevin Abosch releases BANK
events.description: A limited-edition book collating 500 Bitcoin public/private key pairs.

events.title: Colored Coins proposed
events.description: Yoni Assia introduces Colored Coins to represent other assets — an antecedent of NFTs.

events.title: Launch of Casascius Coins
events.description: Mike Caldwell issues physical metal Bitcoins with holographic private keys.

events.title: First ASCII image on the Bitcoin blockchain
events.description: Dan Kaminsky embeds the first tribute and ASCII image, honoring cryptographer Len Sassaman.
